section_miscategorizations.text: He has no history of knee surgery or rehabilitation, and reports no chest pain during physical therapy exercises.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Absence of knee surgery is past medical/surgical history, and lack of chest pain during PT is functional/clinical status, not part of the current chest-pain HPI. Its placement within HPI could mislead the reader into thinking it is directly related to the present illness rather than background history or exam/functional findings.

section_miscategorizations.text: He has a healed scar from a prior knee surgery and no lower extremity edema.
section_miscategorizations.reason: A healed surgical scar and absence of edema are physical exam findings, not patient-reported symptom chronology. Keeping them in HPI blurs the distinction between subjective history and objective exam.

section_miscategorizations.text: He has an enlarged, non-tender thyroid and a carotid bruit on the right side.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Thyroid enlargement and carotid bruit are objective physical exam findings. Documenting them in HPI misplaces exam data in the subjective history section and may confuse readers about whether these were reported by the patient versus observed by the clinician.
